sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.84 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Let$$be a set of primes. We say that a finite group is a$BS_$-group if every conjugacy class in this group any two elements of which generate a$$-subgroup itself generates a$$-subgroup. Is every normal subgroup of a$BS_$-group a$BS_$-group? In the case$2$, an affirmative answer follows from (D.\,O.\,Revin, Siberian Math. J., 52, no.\,2 (2011), 340--347). { D.\,O.\,Revin}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
